title: Global Box Office Report: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple $29.2M Global Debut
text: Sony's 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple launched with $29.2 million globally across 61 territories, securing fourth place. The zombie sequel earned $16.2 million internationally and $13 million domestically from over 10,000 screens. The UK-Ireland led international markets with $4.6 million, followed by Mexico ($1.5M) and Australia ($1M). The film's opening fell short of its predecessor's $60 million debut last June, possibly due to receiving a stricter 18 rating versus the earlier film's 15 rating. Disney's Avatar: Fire And Ash dominated for its fifth consecutive weekend atop the global chart, adding $57.8 million. The blockbuster surpassed $1.3 billion worldwide ($955.3M international, $363.5M domestic), joining the all-time top 25 highest-grossing films. Zootopia 2 reached historic status as the highest-grossing MPA global animated release, exceeding Inside Out 2 with $1.7 billion total. The film became the ninth highest-grossing film ever, overtaking previous records. Paul Feig's The Housemaid climbed to second place with $35.1 million, declining just 4% from its previous weekend. The family thriller now stands at $245.7 million globally across 72 markets.
